title: Colysis thwaitesianus
taxonomy: Colysis thwaitesianus is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Colysis and is classified as a member of the subdivision Pteridophyta, a group of vascular plants. The kingdom to which Colysis thwaitesianus belongs is Plantae, encompassing all types of plants. Within the plant kingdom, Colysis thwaitesianus falls under the subkingdom Viridiplantae, which includes all green plants. This fern species is further classified as a member of the phylum Tracheophyta.

In some cultures, it is believed to be effective in reducing fever and relieving pain. Additionally, the plant is used to treat digestive problems such as diarrhea and indigestion. More research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses and effectiveness of Colysis thwaitesianus.
